questions.q: Thanjavur served as the capital of which medieval South Indian empire?
questions.explanation: Thanjavur (Tanjore) was the capital of the medieval Chola empire.

questions.q: Thanjavur lies in the fertile delta of which river?
questions.explanation: Thanjavur sits in the rich Kaveri delta of Tamil Nadu.

questions.q: In which present-day Indian state is Thanjavur located?
questions.explanation: Thanjavur is a city in the state of Tamil Nadu.

questions.q: Which world-famous Chola temple stands at Thanjavur?
questions.explanation: The Brihadisvara (Rajarajeshwaram) temple, built by Rajaraja I, is at Thanjavur.

questions.q: Thanjavur was an important centre of which activities under the Cholas?
questions.explanation: Thanjavur thrived as a hub of administration, temple-building, and the arts.

questions.q: Which Chola emperor most famously built and beautified Thanjavur?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja Chola I made Thanjavur a magnificent capital and built its great temple.

questions.q: After the Cholas, which dynasties also ruled from or held Thanjavur?
questions.explanation: Thanjavur remained a seat of power under later Nayak and Maratha rulers.

questions.q: Thanjavur is popularly known by which traditional epithet reflecting the rich delta region?
questions.explanation: Thanks to the Kaveri delta's fertility, Thanjavur is often called the 'rice bowl of Tamil Nadu.'

questions.q: Thanjavur is renowned for which classical style of devotional painting using gold foil?
questions.explanation: The gold-embellished Tanjore (Thanjavur) painting style is named after the city.

questions.q: The Brihadisvara temple at Thanjavur is recognised as what?
questions.explanation: The temple is a UNESCO World Heritage Site among the Great Living Chola Temples.

questions.q: Which dynasty captured Thanjavur from the Cholas and ruled it as a Nayak kingdom from the 16th century?
questions.explanation: The Thanjavur Nayaks, originally Vijayanagara governors, ruled the city from the 16th century.

questions.q: Which branch of the Maratha (Bhonsle) family established a kingdom at Thanjavur in the late 17th century?
questions.explanation: A Bhonsle branch founded the Thanjavur Maratha kingdom after Venkoji (Ekoji) took the city.

questions.q: The Maratha ruler Serfoji II of Thanjavur is especially remembered for founding what?
options: A canal network to the Ganges
questions.explanation: Serfoji II expanded the renowned Saraswati Mahal Library, one of Asia's oldest libraries.

questions.q: What is the Tamil name from which 'Thanjavur' is derived?
questions.explanation: Thanjavur is the anglicised form of the Tamil name Tanjavur (Tanjai).

questions.q: Thanjavur is a leading centre for the manufacture of which traditional bobblehead doll?
questions.explanation: Thanjavur is famous for its 'thalaiyatti bommai,' the dancing-head dolls.

questions.q: The Brihadisvara temple at Thanjavur was completed around which year?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I's great temple at Thanjavur was completed around 1010 CE.

questions.q: Before the rise of the medieval Cholas, Thanjavur was held by which minor dynasty that the Cholas displaced?
questions.explanation: Vijayalaya Chola captured Thanjavur from the Muttaraiyar chieftains in the 9th century.

questions.q: Thanjavur is closely tied to which classical dance form, traditionally codified by court musicians there?
questions.explanation: The Thanjavur court nurtured Bharatanatyam, and the 'Thanjavur Quartet' codified its repertoire.

questions.q: The Thanjavur Marathas became tributaries to, and were eventually annexed by, which European power?
questions.explanation: The Thanjavur Maratha state came under British control and was annexed in the 19th century.

questions.q: The musical trinity of Carnatic music flourished in the Thanjavur region during which period's courts?
options: The Maratha and Nayak era (18th-19th centuries)
questions.explanation: The Carnatic 'Trinity' (including Tyagaraja) lived in the Kaveri delta under Maratha-era patronage.

questions.q: What grand royal building, distinct from the temple, was the seat of the Nayaks and Marathas at Thanjavur?
questions.explanation: The Thanjavur Maratha Palace, with its Durbar Hall and library, was the later rulers' residence.

questions.q: The Cholas made Thanjavur their capital after which earlier ruler revived the dynasty in the 9th century?
questions.explanation: Vijayalaya Chola seized Thanjavur and made it the cradle of the revived medieval Chola line.

questions.q: The Saraswati Mahal Library at Thanjavur is notable for preserving manuscripts in which scripts and languages?
options: Tamil, Sanskrit, Telugu, Marathi and more
questions.explanation: The library holds a vast multilingual collection in Tamil, Sanskrit, Telugu, Marathi and other languages.

questions.q: Which Maratha ruler founded the Thanjavur Bhonsle line after seizing the city in 1674-76?
questions.explanation: Venkoji (Ekoji), Shivaji's half-brother, established the Thanjavur Maratha kingdom.

questions.q: Under the Cholas, Thanjavur's administration relied on assemblies of local self-government. What were the village assemblies of brahmadeya villages called?
questions.explanation: Chola local government featured the 'sabha,' the assembly of brahmadeya villages, alongside the 'ur.'

questions.q: The Thanjavur Nayak kingdom rose as a feudatory of which larger empire?
questions.explanation: The Thanjavur Nayaks governed as feudatories of the Vijayanagara Empire.

questions.q: Serfoji II of Thanjavur was educated by, and maintained ties with, which Danish-German missionary?
questions.explanation: The missionary Christian Friedrich Schwartz tutored and supported the young Serfoji II.

questions.q: Which Maratha-era institution at Thanjavur reflects Serfoji II's interest in Western medicine, with a collection of medical manuscripts?
questions.explanation: Serfoji II established the Dhanvantari Mahal, blending Indian and European medical knowledge.

questions.q: The Cholas built a second great temple after Thanjavur at a new capital. Which capital was that?
questions.explanation: Rajendra I shifted the capital to Gangaikonda Cholapuram, though Thanjavur remained a key city.

questions.q: The famous Carnatic composer Tyagaraja, associated with the Thanjavur region, lived chiefly in which delta town?
questions.explanation: Tyagaraja lived in Tiruvaiyaru near Thanjavur, where an annual music festival honours him.
